nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: Citty subcommand `auth` that runs the GitHub device-code login flow and persists the resulting token to disk.

nodes.name: check-usage.ts
nodes.summary: Citty subcommand `check-usage` that authenticates and prints the account's Copilot quota snapshots (premium interactions, chat, completions).

nodes.name: debug.ts
nodes.summary: Citty subcommand `debug` that collects runtime diagnostics (package version, Node/Bun runtime, token presence, paths) and prints them as plain text or JSON.

nodes.name: api-config.ts
nodes.summary: Central source of base URLs and request headers for the Copilot and GitHub APIs, switching between public github.com and an enterprise tenant based on global state.
nodes.languageNotes: All header builders are pure functions of `state`, so enterprise routing is decided at call time rather than at module load.

nodes.name: approval.ts
nodes.summary: Blocks a request on an interactive terminal confirmation, used when the server runs in manual-approve mode.

nodes.name: enterprise.ts
nodes.summary: Shared citty argument definitions and domain normalization for GitHub Enterprise tenants, stripping any scheme before storing the domain in global state.

nodes.name: error.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the HTTPError wrapper carrying an upstream Response and the forwardError helper that converts thrown errors into Hono JSON responses.

nodes.name: model-endpoints.ts
nodes.summary: Local patch: declares which Copilot model id prefixes are served only by the OpenAI Responses API and exports the upstream error code used as the runtime fallback signal.
nodes.languageNotes: A prefix allowlist plus an error-code fallback: the static list is a fast path, and anything it misses is still caught at runtime.

nodes.name: paths.ts
nodes.summary: Resolves the application's on-disk layout (config dir, token files) and ensures those directories and files exist before use.

nodes.name: proxy.ts
nodes.summary: Installs a global undici dispatcher that honours HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY/NO_PROXY per destination URL; a no-op under Bun, which handles proxying natively.
nodes.languageNotes: Caches one ProxyAgent per proxy URL and falls back to a direct Agent, so NO_PROXY hosts skip the tunnel entirely.

nodes.name: rate-limit.ts
nodes.summary: Enforces the optional minimum interval between upstream requests, either sleeping until the window elapses or rejecting the call outright.

nodes.name: shell.ts
nodes.summary: Detects the parent shell (bash, zsh, fish, PowerShell, cmd) and emits a matching environment-variable export script so callers can source the server's settings.
nodes.languageNotes: Shell detection reads the parent process name on Windows and $SHELL elsewhere, so the generated syntax matches the caller's actual shell.

nodes.name: state.ts
nodes.summary: Single mutable module-level State object holding tokens, account type, model catalog, enterprise domain and rate-limit settings shared across the whole server.
nodes.languageNotes: A module-scoped mutable singleton: importers observe live updates without an event bus or dependency injection.

nodes.name: token.ts
nodes.summary: Acquires and refreshes both credentials: the long-lived GitHub OAuth token (device flow, cached on disk) and the short-lived Copilot token refreshed on a timer.

nodes.name: tokenizer.ts
nodes.summary: Reimplements OpenAI's token accounting on top of gpt-tokenizer, counting messages, multimodal content parts, tool calls and JSON-Schema tool definitions to answer token-count requests without calling upstream.
nodes.languageNotes: Per-model constants and a recursive JSON-Schema walker reproduce the provider's documented per-message and per-tool overheads.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Small shared helpers: a promise-based sleep, a nullish guard, and cache warmers that populate the model catalog and VS Code version in global state.

nodes.name: main.ts
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that assembles the citty command tree (auth, start, check-usage, debug) and runs it; this is the file the published bin points at.

nodes.name: handler.ts
nodes.summary: Handles OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions requests: applies rate limiting and manual approval, forwards to Copilot, and streams the reply back as SSE or returns it as JSON.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Hono router mounting the POST /chat/completions endpoint and wrapping the handler in the shared error forwarder.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Hono router exposing POST /embeddings, forwarding the payload straight to the Copilot embeddings service with no translation.

nodes.name: anthropic-types.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript type definitions for the Anthropic Messages API: request payloads, content blocks, tool use, and the streaming event union plus the translator's stream state.

nodes.name: count-tokens-handler.ts
nodes.summary: Serves Anthropic's /v1/messages/count_tokens by translating the payload to OpenAI shape and counting locally with the bundled tokenizer instead of calling upstream.

nodes.name: handler.ts
nodes.summary: Locally patched Anthropic /v1/messages handler that routes each request to either Copilot's /chat/completions or /responses backend, retrying on /responses when the model rejects chat completions, and translates both request and (streamed) response between the Anthropic and OpenAI wire formats.
nodes.languageNotes: Dual-backend dispatch: a static prefix check picks the endpoint up front, and an `unsupported_api_for_model` error triggers a transparent retry on the other backend.

nodes.name: runAuth
nodes.summary: Ensures the config directories exist, then drives the GitHub device-code login and optionally echoes the acquired token.

nodes.name: getDebugInfo
nodes.summary: Gathers the diagnostic snapshot: package version, runtime and platform, token file presence, and resolved application paths.

nodes.name: runDebug
nodes.summary: Entry point of the debug subcommand, selecting plain-text or JSON rendering of the collected diagnostics.

nodes.name: copilotBaseUrl
nodes.summary: Builds the Copilot API base URL for the current account type and enterprise domain.

nodes.name: copilotHeaders
nodes.summary: Assembles the authorization and editor-identification headers Copilot requires, adding the vision flag when the request carries images.

nodes.name: githubHeaders
nodes.summary: Builds the GitHub REST API headers carrying the stored OAuth token and editor identification.

nodes.name: awaitApproval
nodes.summary: Prompts on the terminal and throws an HTTPError rejection if the operator declines the pending request.

nodes.name: normalizeDomain
nodes.summary: Strips the URL scheme and trailing slashes from a user-supplied enterprise domain.

nodes.name: applyEnterpriseDomain
nodes.summary: Normalizes the enterprise domain argument and records it in global state so URL builders switch to the tenant endpoints.

nodes.name: HTTPError
nodes.summary: Error subclass that carries the originating fetch Response so handlers can inspect the upstream status and body.

nodes.name: forwardError
nodes.summary: Logs a thrown error and turns it into a Hono JSON response, preserving the upstream status code and body when the error wraps an HTTP response.

nodes.name: usesResponsesApi
nodes.summary: Returns true when a model id matches a prefix known to be served only by the OpenAI Responses API.

nodes.name: githubTokenPath
nodes.summary: Returns the on-disk token file path, namespaced per enterprise domain so tenants do not share credentials.

nodes.name: ensurePaths
nodes.summary: Creates the application directory and token file if missing, with owner-only permissions.

nodes.name: initProxyFromEnv
nodes.summary: Registers a global undici dispatcher that picks a cached ProxyAgent or a direct Agent per request URL according to the proxy environment variables.

nodes.name: checkRateLimit
nodes.summary: Compares the elapsed time since the last request against the configured interval, then either waits or throws a rate-limit error.

nodes.name: getShell
nodes.summary: Identifies the invoking shell from the parent process on Windows or the SHELL environment variable elsewhere.

nodes.name: generateEnvScript
nodes.summary: Renders environment-variable assignments (and an optional command) in the syntax of the detected shell.

nodes.name: setupCopilotToken
nodes.summary: Exchanges the GitHub token for a short-lived Copilot token and schedules a timer to refresh it before expiry.

nodes.name: setupGitHubToken
nodes.summary: Loads the cached GitHub token or, when absent or forced, runs the full device-code flow and writes the new token to disk.

nodes.name: getTokenCount
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point that splits a payload into input and output messages and returns token totals for both, including tool definitions.

nodes.name: numTokensForTools
nodes.summary: Sums the token cost of every tool definition plus the fixed per-request tool overhead.

nodes.name: getTokenizerFromModel
nodes.summary: Maps a model id to the gpt-tokenizer encoding used to count its tokens.

nodes.name: calculateMessageTokens
nodes.summary: Counts one chat message, covering plain text, multimodal content parts, tool calls and the per-message structural overhead.

nodes.name: calculateParameterTokens
nodes.summary: Recursively walks a JSON-Schema property (nested objects, arrays, enums, descriptions) accumulating its token cost inside a tool definition.

nodes.name: cacheModels
nodes.summary: Fetches the Copilot model catalog once and stores it in global state for later lookups.

nodes.name: cacheVSCodeVersion
nodes.summary: Resolves the VS Code version string used in editor identification headers and caches it in global state.

nodes.name: handleCompletion
nodes.summary: Rate-limits and optionally gates the request, forwards it to Copilot, then returns JSON or relays the SSE stream verbatim.

nodes.name: handleCountTokens
nodes.summary: Translates the Anthropic count-tokens payload to OpenAI shape and returns the locally computed input token total.

nodes.name: handleCompletion
nodes.summary: Anthropic /v1/messages entry point: picks the Copilot backend for the requested model and transparently retries on /responses when /chat/completions rejects it.

nodes.name: isUnsupportedApiError
nodes.summary: Detects the upstream `unsupported_api_for_model` rejection by reading a clone of the error response, leaving the original body readable for error forwarding.

nodes.name: handleWithChatCompletions
nodes.summary: Runs the Anthropic request through the /chat/completions backend, translating the payload out and the JSON or SSE reply back into Anthropic events.

nodes.name: handleWithResponses
nodes.summary: Runs the Anthropic request through the /responses backend, translating the payload out and the JSON or SSE reply back into Anthropic events.
